Let me call the first three runs “ordinary,” not because they didn’t have enough spikes - they did - but because they didn’t have the long term support I will talk about shortly. As you can see, gains have consistently fallen - which is due to, among other things, the much higher price of coins.

Let me chart this:

Explainer:

  • The blue line shows the absolute percentage gain after each halving.

  • The red dashed line shows how much smaller each gain was compared to the prior halving cycle.

You can see that while every halving triggered a big rally, the magnitude of gains keeps declining sharply from cycle to cycle.

Now, let me make two assumptions - a) this run will last 36 months instead of the usual 18. b) this run will continue at the same rate as we are seeing this year, compounded monthly (~3.47% compounded monthly for 18 months is 85%).

I am making the first assumption because this time, we have a President who is doing many things to regularize crypto, both at policy levels and at a personal business level. In terms of the first, in March, President Trump announced the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and Digital Asset Stockpile, which is a US government level backing for crypto assets helpful for investor confidence. The policy also puts price pressure on coins because it “theoretically turns the government into a structural non-seller.” Also, Trump has lifted regulatory burdens, appointing a crypto czar, reversing SEC enforcement actions, and proposed enabling 401(k) retirement investments into crypto. This has unlocked potential access to $8.7 trillion in U.S. retirement assets.

At a personal business level, Trump floated the first Presidential meme token - the $TRUMP - hosted on Solana blockchain. Although its rally did not last very long, Trump’s involvement, coupled with the fact that JD Vance, his Vice President, was the first VP to hold bitcoins, helped secularize the crypto trend in the early months of his Presidency.

At the same time, the short-lived $TRUMP rally highlights reputational risks. If it is seen as a pump-and-dump, that could undermine investor confidence rather than bolster it. This risk tempers the otherwise strong bullish narrative.

Given all of the above, I argue that President Trump’s presidency will continue supporting crypto at various levels. During his first presidency, Trump was critical of crypto. This time, his support will anchor the bull run. I give it 36 months instead of the usual 18 months, so, in effect, another 18 months from today.

As to my second assumption, again, this is also following from Trump’s support, as well as from European regulation - the MiCA - and worldwide governmental and institutional interest in bitcoin. Between 2020 and 2021, the world was involved in multiple wars and a pandemic, and there was no major high-level governmental support for crypto. Yet it managed to have a nice bull run at a certain rate. This time, I think it's a fair assumption that the current bull run will be able to continue the entire 36 months at at least the same rate we see in this cycle.

Here’s a chart showing what happens to bitcoin given my two assumptions:

Explanation

  • Blue bars: Historical halving peaks (log scale).

  • Red line: Projected 2024 cycle extended to 36 months, starting at ~$65k post-halving.

  • Growth continues at the same approximate rate as the current cycle, i.e., ~3.47% .

Outcomes

  • At 18 months (usual halving cycle) → BTC reaches about $120K.

  • At 36 months (extended Trump + MiCA + Fed easing scenario) → BTC could theoretically push to ~$222k.

Now, in August of 2025, we are at 18 months of the halving cycle, and BTC is now at a little less than that projection - at $113k. I would account for that by saying that Trump’s positive effects are yet to be factored in. If we continue with the projection, at the same rate as this cycle so far, we arrive at $222k for bitcoin at 36 months - nearly double what it is today. [Note - These are highly speculative scenarios, not predictive. I am not at all claiming here that bitcoin will reach that figure in 2026. I am simply illustrating a best case scenario.]

Indeed, an expert recently painted a much rosier picture - that bitcoin will go to $1 million - for pretty much the same reason - Trump:

“Winter is not coming back,” commented Saylor [Executive Chairman and CEO of Strategy Michael Saylor]. “We are past that phase. If Bitcoin is not going to zero, it is going to $1 million. The President of the United States is determined. He supports Bitcoin, the cabinet supports Bitcoin, Scott Bessent supports Bitcoin, Paul Atkins is shown himself to be an enthusiastic believer of Bitcoin and digital assets… Bitcoin has gotten through its riskiest period.”

Trading BITU makes sense now - with caveats

BITU is designed for moments like this, when we are late in a strong bull run, and we want to take a second or a late shot at the spike without getting into margins and futures. It offers 2x daily exposure to the Bloomberg Bitcoin Index. It uses swaps and futures, and if you do it right and the bull run lasts, you could double your gains.

BITU has two sorts of caveats, one centered around whether the bull run lasts, where the downside is absolute and unavoidable, and two whether the run is volatile, i.e., with up and down swings, or is smooth - where the downside is manageable with active trading.

BITU’s cumulative price performance depends not on the net change in bitcoin’s price, but its actual path. The only time BITU is very profitable for investors is if bitcoin goes up on a straight path during the holding period. If the share price makes a zigzag movement, then, even if the net change is positive, BITU may go down due to the volatility’s erosive effects. As Proshares itself notes - “Smaller index gains/losses and higher index volatility contribute to returns worse than the Daily Target.”

The other general drawback is Contango, or higher Futures Roll Costs. In a contango market, futures cost higher than spot prices. The formula is, Futures Price = Spot Price × (1 + r – y)^T. where r = risk-free rate (financing cost), y = any yield the asset provides, T = time. Since there is no yield here, high demand causing higher financing cost makes the futures price higher than the spot price. Now, BITU uses futures to achieve its 2x leverage, so it incurs some of these costs in a contango market. While BITU’s swap strategies might offset some of the loss from the carry cost, this is something to keep in mind.

Despite the above general concerns, BITU has given us over 110% total return in the last one year, compared to BTC’s 85%, as we can see in the table below:

However, it has lagged during choppy periods, proving once again that it is not the right instrument for everybody.

How to trade BITU

BITU is a potent tactical instrument when Bitcoin is in a steady uptrend. I have argued for why we may see a much longer uptrend this time. If you believe that Trump’s policies and other macrotrends will support bitcoin in a mature manner this time, giving us a long-term bull market, here’s how I would trade BITU, considering all those caveats I discussed.

Strategic Setup

  1. Entry Point—Tactical Deployment Only:

    • I will look for sustained momentum but skip local highs. I will wait for short-term post-dip rebounds or technical breakouts.

  2. Position Sizing and Risk Controls:

    • I will only trade 5–10% of my total crypto allocation, due to high volatility and compounding risk.

    • I will set a tight stop-loss (e.g., –10–15%), knowing that a single bad day in BTC can result in ~–20–30% move in BITU. I will also watch my trade continuously while it executes.

  3. Target Management & Scaling Out:

    • Scaled exits: I will take profits at increments of 20%, allowing the remaining position to ride a strong trend higher.

    • I will monitor BTC price action continuously; if momentum deteriorates, I will exit fully regardless of returns.

Other things I will keep in mind are to avoid carry costs and tax complexities. BITU’s monthly dividends are futures-based gains taxed 60/40. Also, and this is important, I will enter and exit my positions for no longer than a week, preferably even less.

[Note: Bitcoin’s volatility makes holding BITU for more than a few days hazardous. For example, in early March 2024, spot BTC dropped from $90,547 on March 3 to $78,267 on March 10 — a –13.5% slide in just one week. Over the exact same period, BITU fell from $41.84 to $31.04, a –26% decline, which is almost exactly the 2× magnification you would expect — plus a bit more due to daily compounding drag. This illustrates why I recommend treating BITU as a tactical trade lasting only a few days — never more than a week — so that leverage captures momentum bursts without letting volatility drag wipe out capital.]

Risks to my thesis

There are several risks to my bull thesis. There could be a regulatory reversal, where Trump’s policies could shift. I do not see a lot of reasons for a broad policy shift because Trump has just started getting bullish on crypto, but it could happen. Or Europe’s MiCA implementation might face pushback, or other global institutions may not take it up as strongly as my thesis expects.

There could be macro shocks in the form of a surprise hawkish Fed stance on interest rates, which could hurt crypto. Something as sudden as a weekend flush could wipe 30-40% off BITU in one session. That could make its underlying swap/futures produce slippage, magnifying the risk.

Another off-the-grid risk factor is President Trump’s personal involvement in crypto. I noted that it had produced some enthusiasm. However, as $TRUMP fell, it could well be that there could be reputational risks with the entire sector.

Lastly, we must not forget that my thesis also depends a little on the lack of choppiness in the trend. If it remains choppy and I do not trade BITU as actively as I should, I will see losses as a compounding effect sets in.

Conclusion

Halving-related scarcity, Trump’s positive attitude, MiCA, Federal easing - all have converged to create a unique environment for a more sustained bull run in crypto this time. In such a scenario, BITU offers a high-beta tactical tool for active traders to magnify their bitcoin gains. The key is discipline - right entry, right management, and right exit.
